summary: Added CI coverage for both the normal map-generating build and a NON_MATCHING build. The Makefile gained a NON_MATCHING option that adds -DNON_MATCHING to CFLAGS and skips the SHA1 checksum when enabled, while GitHub Actions now uses a matrix over GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1 make invocations.
what_changed: Updated .github/workflows/build.yml to add a build strategy matrix with makeflags values GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1.
Changed the CI make step from a hard-coded `GENERATE_MAP=1 make -j$(nproc)` command to `make -j$(nproc) ${{ matrix.makeflags }}` so both matrix variants run.
When `NON_MATCHING=1`, the Makefile appends `-DNON_MATCHING` to CFLAGS.
When `NON_MATCHING=1`, the default target prints `Skipping checksum for non-matching build.` instead of running `$(SHA1SUM) -c $(TARGET).sha1`.
The PR body only provided a successful GitHub Actions run link as supporting evidence.
smart_moves: Used a CI matrix rather than duplicating workflow jobs, keeping the workflow small while exercising both build configurations.
Kept NON_MATCHING disabled by default with `NON_MATCHING ?= 0`, preserving normal matching-build behavior for local users and existing CI assumptions.
Skipped the checksum only for non-matching builds, which is necessary because NON_MATCHING code may intentionally produce a DOL that does not match the target SHA1.
Introduced NON_MATCHING as a compiler define through Makefile CFLAGS, creating a centralized switch for source-level non-matching code paths.
decomp_lessons: If non-matching code paths are supported by the source tree, CI should build them so they do not silently rot while the matching configuration stays green.
A non-matching DOL should not be subjected to the same SHA1 verification as the matching artifact; gate checksum validation on the matching-build configuration.
Build flags such as GENERATE_MAP and NON_MATCHING are best exposed as Makefile variables with safe defaults so CI and local developers can opt in explicitly.
Small CI matrices are useful for decomp projects because they can validate alternate compilation modes without making separate workflows or changing default build behavior.
naming_conventions: Makefile feature switches use uppercase snake-case variables such as `GENERATE_MAP` and `NON_MATCHING`.
The C preprocessor define mirrors the Makefile variable name: `-DNON_MATCHING`.
The GitHub Actions matrix key was named `makeflags`, directly describing the values appended to the make command.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR did not introduce assembly-level matching tactics directly.
The build-system tactic was to permit `NON_MATCHING` source branches to compile without requiring the resulting DOL to match the canonical SHA1.
The normal CI build still runs with `GENERATE_MAP=1`, preserving map generation and checksum validation for the matching path.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the PR title and the successful run link.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add or modify source code guarded by `#ifdef NON_MATCHING`.
Find later CI changes involving `GENERATE_MAP`, `NON_MATCHING`, or checksum skipping.
Check whether subsequent build workflows continued using the `makeflags` matrix pattern.
Look for issues where non-matching build failures were caught by this CI addition.
